behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not only local classification logic; it depends on how real git represents renames before categorization. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a mocked diff can hide rename-detection edge cases from actual git behavior. SYSTEM TRIAD (1): verify real external happy-path contract from the repo read path and CLI output. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/change categorization (no AI/ML output, no multi-agent sequence), so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline, perform a true rename, run the CLI as a subprocess, parse stdout report, and assert the renamed count increments while added/deleted do not increment for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: binary diff handling often depends on real git output and subprocess/stdout behavior. MOCK-HIDE: stubs usually provide sanitized text and miss real binary markers/encoding quirks. SYSTEM TRIAD (1): must verify real external response shape for binary entries in produced report. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML judgment and no chained agentic steps in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file change (and optionally mixed text+binary), run CLI via subprocess, assert exit code is success, no crash, and stdout report includes binary file in the correct count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this edge case depends on real repository state and how git commands fail/return before first commit. MOCK-HIDE: mocked git responses can mask real initial-repo error semantics. SYSTEM TRIAD (2): verify real external error-path contract is handled and translated to empty report. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run CLI as subprocess, assert successful exit (or documented non-error exit), and stdout is the expected empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: ROUND-TRIP EFFECT RULE / SYSTEM TRIAD (3): correctness requires proving externally visible report counts match real repo history via readback. MOCK-MISS: cross-module handoff (history extraction -> classification -> report rendering) is central. REAL-INFRA: must observe real repo history, not synthetic fixtures only. Rejected higher levels: behavior is deterministic repository accounting, not AI/ML output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across several commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert stdout counts equal counts independently computed from real git history readback.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged semantics depend on real git index/worktree state. MOCK-HIDE: fakes often miss subtle index behavior differences. SYSTEM TRIAD (1): verify real external output contract equivalence across two real repo states. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML model decision and no multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI subprocess for each state, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
